qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Persisted extension auth token enables silent relay attachment and browser-session hijack by a low-privileged local attacker
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets a local browser-extension token bypass and attacker-controlled relay attachment flow, while the advisory targets missing Origin-header validation on incoming connections to the local MCP server that enables DNS rebinding from a victim browser.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's server-side root cause. The only qualifying finding is a local extension auth-token bypass and relay hijack, not the advisory's missing Origin validation on incoming MCP server connections that enables DNS rebinding and unauthorized tool invocation.
evidence: The advisory summary and details describe a DNS rebinding issue in @playwright/mcp caused by failure to validate the Origin header on incoming connections to a locally running MCP server.
The session input manifest lists exactly one final qualifying finding: extension_local_token_relay_hijack.

That finding requires low-privileged local access to read a persisted extension token and abuses the extension's connect.html/background/relayConnection flow, which is a different component and trust boundary from server-side incoming request validation.
No final qualifying finding in the run attributes exploitation to missing Origin checks, DNS rebinding, or unauthorized invocation of MCP tool endpoints through incoming local-server requests.
